comments.author: @World_Untold_321
comments.text: Such a magical place, thanks for sharing this!

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: Thank you so much! Tibet truly is a magical place with breathtaking landscapes and a unique cultural heritage.

comments.author: @EpicAdventureWorld
comments.text: Would you actually visit Tibet if you had the chance, or is it too remote for you?

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: I would absolutely visit Tibet if I had the chance. Its remoteness is part of what makes it so fascinating, the mountains, monasteries, culture, and silence feel like something you can’t experience anywhere else.

comments.author: @yogaguru108
comments.text: Some very good video of Tibet and the interesting locations. I wonder why though there are several videos of Mt Everest from the Nepal side, not Tibet. Also, several videos show prayer wheels going counter clockwise, very much against tradition of them going clockwise.

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: Thank you for taking the time to look closely and point this out. You're right, these details are very important, especially for a place of such cultural significance as Tibet. We will pay more attention to the Everest shots and the orientation of the prayer wheels, and will be more careful with these details in future videos.
